essays:
```latex
\text{MATRIX MULTIPLICATION:} \\
[1 \ 2] \quad [5 \ 6] \quad [5 \ 12] \\
[3 \ 4] \times [7 \ 8] = [21 \ 32] \\
\text{I MULTIPLIED THE NUMBERS THAT ARE IN THE SAME POSITION IN BOTH MATRICES,} \\
\text{so } 1*5=5, 2*6=12, 3*7=21, 4*8=32.
```

$$\text{CONVOLUTION:}$$
$$[1 \quad 0] \quad [4 \quad 5]$$
$$[0 \quad -1] * [6 \quad 7] = 4$$
$$\text{I MULTIPLIED THE TOP-LEFT FILTER VALUE BY THE TOP-LEFT PATCH VALUE,}$$
$$1 * 4 = 4 \text{ AND THAT GAVE ME THE ANSWER}$$

```latex
\begin{verbatim}
DEF TABLE(N):
  FOR I IN RANGE(N):
    FOR J IN RANGE(N):
      PRINT(I * J)

THIS LOOPS THROUGH I AND J STARTING FROM 0 UP TO N AND PRINTS EACH
MULTIPLICATION AS IT GOES.
\end{verbatim}
```
